(REFERENCE 1)
English: My forbears, the old men and women folk, were of the Mexican People Clan. With great hardship they came back from the place called Fort Sumner, packing their goods on their backs, and afterward lived here at Lukachukai.
1
English: My grandfather, a man by the name of White Mexican Clansman, and another man called The Son of Former Sweathouse, were the only men. They and several womenfolk worked hard on me.
Navajo: Shichei ńt’éé’ Naakai Dine’é Łigaii wolyéé ńt’éé’ dóó ła’ dó’ Diné Táchéhéni’ Biye’ wolyéé ńt’éé’ ’éí shį́į́ t’éiyá diné nilį́įgo dóó sáanii dó’ díkwíí shį́į́ kwii shaa naanish bííghą́ą́’
[Shichei (nv): my grandfather (en): shi- → 1.SG.POSS: chei → maternal.grandfather | ńt’éé’ (nv): past (en): ńt’éé’ → PST | Naakai (nv): Mexican (en): Naakai → Mexican | Dine’é (nv): people (en): dine’é → people | Łigaii (nv): white (en): łigaii → white | wolyéé (nv): is called (en): wo- → DIR: l- → L: yéé → call.NEU.IMPF | ńt’éé’ (nv): past (en): ńt’éé’ → PST | dóó (nv): and (en): dóó → and | ła’ (nv): one (en): ła’ → one | dó’ (nv): also (en): dó’ → also | Diné (nv): people (en): diné → person | Táchéhéni’ (nv): former sweathouse (en): Táchéhéni’ → Former.Sweathouse | Biye’ (nv): his son (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: ye’ → son | wolyéé (nv): he is called (en): wo- → DIR: l- → L: yéé → call.NEU.IMPF | ńt’éé’ (nv): past (en): ńt’éé’ → PST | ’éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | shį́į́ (nv): probably (en): shį́į́ → probably | t’éiyá (nv): only (en): t’éiyá → only | diné (nv): people (en): diné → person | nilį́įgo (nv): being (en): ni- → TH: lį́į → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | dóó (nv): and (en): dóó → and | sáanii (nv): women (en): sáanii → women | dó’ (nv): also (en): dó’ → also | díkwíí (nv): several (en): díkwíí → several | shį́į́ (nv): probably (en): shį́į́ → probably | kwii (nv): here about (en): kwii → here.about | shaa (nv): on me (en): sh- → 1.SG.POSS: aa → to | naanish (nv): they work (en): naa- → CONT: nish → work.CONT.IMPF | bííghą́ą́’ (nv): it killed them (en): b- → 3.BI: íí- → 3.YI.PERF: ghą́ą́’ → kill.DU/PL.PERF]

2
English: They were to go by way of Lukachukai, Tsaile, and Crystal, and on over to Fort Defiance.
Navajo: Nagháí Lók’a’jíkai góne’ dóó Tsééhílį́į́ góne’ dóó Sǫ’ Siláágóó dóó ńléí Tséhootsooíjį’
[Nagháí (nv): there (en): nagháí → there | Lók’a’jíkai (nv): Lukachukai (en): Lók’a’jíkai → Lukachukai | góne’ (nv): inside (en): góne’ → inside | dóó (nv): and (en): dóó → and | Tsééhílį́į́ (nv): Tsaile (en): Tsééhílį́į́ → Tsaile | góne’ (nv): inside (en): góne’ → inside | dóó (nv): and (en): dóó → and | Sǫ’ Siláágóó (nv): Crystal (en): Sǫ’ Siláágóó → Crystal | dóó (nv): and (en): dóó → and | ńléí (nv): over there (en): ńléí → over.there | Tséhootsooíjį’ (nv): to Fort Defiance (en): Tséhootsooí → Fort.Defiance: =jį’ → =up.to]

3
English: Wait.
Navajo: T’aháloo
[T’aháloo (nv): wait (en): t’aháloo → wait]

4
English: The two of them built the trading post at the place called Blue Clay Point.
Navajo: ’Áko ’éí yaa ndilt’éego Bis Dootł’izh Deez’áhí hoolyéegi naalyéhé bá hooghan ’áhoolaa
[’Áko (nv): so (en): ’áko → so | ’éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | yaa (nv): under (en): yaa → under | ndilt’éego (nv): being two (en): n- → two: di- → TH: l- → L: t’ée → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | Bis Dootł’izh Deez’áhí (nv): Blue Clay Point (en): Bis Dootł’izh Deez’áhí → Blue.Clay.Point | hoolyéegi (nv): where it is called (en): h- → AR: oo- → DIR: l- → L: yée → call.NEU.IMPF: =gi → =at.SP | naalyéhé bá hooghan (nv): trading post (en): naalyéhé bá hooghan → trading.post | ’áhoolaa (nv): it was built (en): ’á- → TH: h- → AR: oo- → 3.YI.PERF: laa → make.MOM3.PERF.PAS]

5
English: Soldiers came from Fort Defiance.
Navajo: Tséhootsooídę́ę́’ siláago níná
[Tséhootsooídę́ę́’ (nv): from Fort Defiance (en): Tséhootsooí → Fort.Defiance: =dę́ę́’ → =from | siláago (nv): policemen (en): siláago → policeman | níná (nv): they arrived (en): ní- → 3.NI.PERF: ná → move.PERF]

Reference: My forbears, the old men and women folk, were of the Mexican People Clan. With great hardship they came back from the place called Fort Sumner, packing their goods on their backs, and afterward lived here at Lukachukai.

(REFERENCE 2)
English: Here they began life anew.
1
English: and he got through.
Navajo: ’Éí shį́į́ haayáá lá jiní
[’Éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | shį́į́ (nv): probably (en): shį́į́ → probably | haayáá (nv): he came up (en): ha- → TH: a- → 3.YI.ZERO.PERF: yáá → go.SG.PERF | lá (nv): ! (en): lá → ! | jiní (nv): they say (en): ji- → ANIM: ní → say.NEU.IMPF]

2
English: because he protected me.
Navajo: Shich’ą́ą́h ’ííyá nisingo
[Shich’ą́ą́h (nv): in front of me (en): shi- → 1.SG.POSS: ch’ą́ą́h → in.front.of | ’ííyá (nv): he went (en): ’- → away: íí- → 3.YI.PERF: yá → go.SG.PERF | nisingo (nv): that I know (en): ni- → TH: zin → know.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB]

3
English: So he set out for there.
Navajo: Aadóó ’ákǫ́ǫ́ dashdiiyá
[Aadóó (nv): and then (en): ’aadóó → and.then | ’ákǫ́ǫ́ (nv): there (en): ’ákǫ́ǫ́ → there | dashdiiyá (nv): he went up (en): da- → TH: sh- → ANIM: d- → TH: ii- → 3.YI.ZERO.PERF: yá → go.SG.PERF]

4
English: Then we started to follow the sheep again.
Navajo: ’Aadóó dibé bikéé’ dah náádiit’áázh
[’Aadóó (nv): and then (en): ’aadóó → and.then | dibé (nv): sheep (en): dibé → sheep | bikéé’ (nv): behind them (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: kéé’ → behind | dah (nv): off (en): dah → off | náádiit’áázh (nv): we started to go again (en): náá- → SITR: d- → TH: ii- → 1.DU/PL.YI.ZERO.PERF: t- → D: ’áázh → go.DU.PERF]

5
English: So we went in.
Navajo: T’áá ’áko wóne’é yah ’iit’áázh jiní
[T’áá ’áko (nv): all right! (en): t’áá ’áko → all.right! | wóne’é (nv): inside (en): wóne’é → inside | yah (nv): into (en): yah → into | ’iit’áázh (nv): we two went (en): ’- → away: ii- → 1.DU/PL.YI.PERF: t- → D: ’áázh → go.DU.PERF | jiní (nv): he said (en): ji- → ANIM: ní → say.NEU.IMPF]

Reference: Here they began life anew.

(REFERENCE 3)
English: Then from their home at a place called Spring in the Clay they started moving over and across yonder mountain.
1
English: Then we followed the ridge up over Black Mountain.
Navajo: ’Aadóó ’eii Dziłíjiin dah sitą́ t’áá bikáá’góó náániikai
[’Aadóó (nv): and then (en): ’aadóó → and.then | ’eii (nv): that (en): ’eii → that | Dziłíjiin (nv): Black Mountain (en): Dziłíjiin → Black.Mountain | dah sitą́ (nv): ridge (en): dah → up: si- → 3.SI.PERF: tą́ → be.placed.SSO.PERF | t’áá (nv): quite (en): t’áá → PCL | bikáá’góó (nv): on the top (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: káá’ → on.top: =góó → =towards | náániikai (nv): we went again (en): náá- → SITR: nii- → 1.DU/PL.NI.PERF: kai → go.PL.PERF]

2
English: And some moved over to Black Mountain.
Navajo: Ła’ Dziłíjiin bída’iyiinééh
[Ła’ (nv): one (en): ła’ → one | Dziłíjiin (nv): Black Mountain (en): Dziłíjiin → Black.Mountain | bída’iyiinééh (nv): they are moving one after another with their household against it (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: í → against: da- → DISTR: ’i- → TH: ii- → SER: nééh → move.IMPF]

3
English: When they moved over to the mountains they went in wagons.
Navajo: Ńléí dził bída’iyiináago ’ákǫ́ǫ́ tsinaabąąs da bee da’níná
[Ńléí (nv): over there (en): ńléí → over.there | dził (nv): mountain (en): dził → mountain | bída’iyiináago (nv): after they moved up against it (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: í → against: da- → DISTR: ’i- → TH: y- → SER: ii- → 3.YI.ZERO.PERF: náa → move.PERF: =go → =SUB | ’ákǫ́ǫ́ (nv): there (en): ’ákǫ́ǫ́ → there | tsinaabąąs (nv): wagon (en): tsinaabąąs → wagon | da (nv): including (en): da → including | bee (nv): by means of (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: ee → with | da’níná (nv): they moved (en): da- → DISTR: ’- → TH: ní- → 3.NI.PERF: ná → move.PERF]

4
English: The two of them built the trading post at the place called Blue Clay Point.
Navajo: ’Áko ’éí yaa ndilt’éego Bis Dootł’izh Deez’áhí hoolyéegi naalyéhé bá hooghan ’áhoolaa
[’Áko (nv): so (en): ’áko → so | ’éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | yaa (nv): under (en): yaa → under | ndilt’éego (nv): being two (en): n- → two: di- → TH: l- → L: t’ée → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | Bis Dootł’izh Deez’áhí (nv): Blue Clay Point (en): Bis Dootł’izh Deez’áhí → Blue.Clay.Point | hoolyéegi (nv): where it is called (en): h- → AR: oo- → DIR: l- → L: yée → call.NEU.IMPF: =gi → =at.SP | naalyéhé bá hooghan (nv): trading post (en): naalyéhé bá hooghan → trading.post | ’áhoolaa (nv): it was built (en): ’á- → TH: h- → AR: oo- → 3.YI.PERF: laa → make.MOM3.PERF.PAS]

5
English: So they moved up onto the mountain.
Navajo: ’Áko diné lą’í dził yighą́ą́’ hadahineesdzood
[’Áko (nv): so (en): ’áko → so | diné (nv): people (en): diné → person | lą’í (nv): many (en): lą’ → be.many.NEU.IMPF: =í → =NMLZ | dził (nv): mountain (en): dził → mountain | yighą́ą́’ (nv): its back (en): yi- → 3.YI.POSS: ghą́ą́’ → back | hadahineesdzood (nv): they drove up one after another (en): ha- → TH: da- → DISTR: hi- → SER: n- → TH: ees- → 3.SI.PERF: d- → D: yood → drive.PERF]

Reference: Then from their home at a place called Spring in the Clay they started moving over and across yonder mountain.

(REFERENCE 4)
English: While they were making this move they stopped for the night, and it is said that I was born where they halted.
1
English: As they were taking him outside I crawled and squeezed myself out among them.
Navajo: Kǫ́ǫ́ yił yijahgo t’áá ’atát’ah ch’ínísh’na’
[Kǫ́ǫ́ (nv): through here (en): kǫ́ǫ́ → through.here | yił (nv): with them (en): y- → 3.YI.POSS: ił → with | yijahgo (nv): while they were running (en): yi- → 3.PROG: jah → run.PL.PROG: =go → =SUB | t’áá (nv): just (en): t’áá → PCL | ’atát’ah (nv): among (en): ’a- → TH: tát’ah → among | ch’ínísh’na’ (nv): I crawled out (en): ch’í- → out: nísh- → 1.SG.NI.PERF: ’- → D: na’ → crawl.PERF]

2
English: Then he went back to where the noise came from, and found a baby lying there, it is said.
Navajo: ’Aadóó nát’ą́ą́ ’áajį’ dah nízhdiidzáá ńt’éé’ kǫ́ǫ́ ’awéé’ léi’ sitį́ jiní
[’Aadóó (nv): and then (en): ’aadóó → and.then | nát’ą́ą́ (nv): returning back (en): nát’ą́ą́’ → back | ’áajį’ (nv): to that point (en): ’áa → there: =jį’ → =up.to | dah (nv): up (en): dah → up | nízhdiidzáá (nv): he started to go back (en): ní- → REV: zh- → ANIM: d- → INC: ii- → 3.YI.ZERO.PERF: d- → D: záá → go.SG.PERF | ńt’éé’ (nv): past (en): ńt’éé’ → PST | kǫ́ǫ́ (nv): through here (en): kǫ́ǫ́ → through.here | ’awéé’ (nv): baby (en): ’awéé’ → baby | léi’ (nv): a certain (en): léi’ → a.certain | sitį́ (nv): it laid (en): si- → 3.SI.PERF: tį́ → lie.ANIM.PERF | jiní (nv): they say (en): ji- → ANIM: ní → say.NEU.IMPF]

3
English: It is said that, in the past, the people really made it a point to teach one another.
Navajo: T’ah nahdę́ę́’ diné t’áá yéego nda’ahinitin ńt’éé’ daaní
[T’ah nahdę́ę́’ (nv): in former times (en): t’ah nahdę́ę́’ → in.former.times | diné (nv): people (en): diné → person | t’áá (nv): just (en): t’áá → PCL | yéego (nv): diligently (en): yéego → diligently | nda’ahinitin (nv): they teach one another (en): n- → CONT: da- → DISTR: ’ahi- → REC: ni- → TH: tin → move.SSO.CONT.IMP | ńt’éé’ (nv): past (en): ńt’éé’ → PST | daaní (nv): they say (en): daa- → DISTR: ní → say.NEU.IMPF]

4
English: It was said that they would never return.
Navajo: Doo nízhdookah da
[Doo (nv): not (en): doo → not | nízhdookah (nv): they will come back (en): ní- → REV: zh- → ANIM: d- → FUT: oo- → 3.PROG: kah → go.PL.FUT | da (nv): not (en): da → not]

5
English: It was said that the first word of this had come from up north.
Navajo: Nagháí náhookǫsdę́ę’go ’áłtsé hane’ yit’ą́ hodoo’niid
[Nagháí (nv): there (en): nagháí → there | náhookǫsdę́ę’go (nv): from the north (en): náhookǫs → north: =dę́ę’ → =from: =go → =SUB | ’áłtsé (nv): first (en): ’áłtsé → first | hane’ (nv): word (en): hane’ → story | yit’ą́ (nv): it was sent (en): yi- → 3.YI.PERF: t- → D: ’ą́ → move.SRO.PERF | hodoo’niid (nv): it was said (en): ho- → AR: d- → TH: oo- → 3.YI.PERF: ’- → D: niid → say.PERF]

Reference: While they were making this move they stopped for the night, and it is said that I was born where they halted.

(REFERENCE 5)
English: Let’s see, I believe it was in the month of October
1
English: I had a hard time during that period.
Navajo: Shí ’éí ’ákwe’é shich’į’ ninááhwiis’náá’
[Shí (nv): I (en): shí → 1.SG | ’éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | ’ákwe’é (nv): right there (en): ’ákwe’é → right.there | shich’į’ (nv): toward me (en): shi- → 1.SG.POSS: ch’į’ → toward | ninááhwiis’náá’ (nv): things have quaked again (en): ni- → CONT: náá- → SITR: hw- → AR: ii- → SER: s- → 3.SI.PERF: ’- → D: náá’ → quake.PERF]

2
English: Wait.
Navajo: T’aháloo
[T’aháloo (nv): wait (en): t’aháloo → wait]

3
English: I remember this event.
Navajo: Shí ’éí díí ’áhóót’įįdígíí t’áá bénáshniih
[Shí (nv): I (en): shí → 1.SG | ’éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | díí (nv): this (en): díí → this | ’áhóót’įįdígíí (nv): what happened (en): ’á- → TH: h- → AR: óó- → 3.YI.PERF: t- → D: ’įįd → do.PERF: =ígíí → =PRES.REF | t’áá (nv): just (en): t’áá → PCL | bénáshniih (nv): I remember (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: é → against: ná- → REV: sh- → 1.SG.ZERO.IMPF: niih → be.aware.of.DUR.IMPF]

4

Navajo: Shí nisingo baa ntséskees

5
English: “Let me see,”
Navajo: Haa’ísh do’
[Haa’ísh (nv): where? (en): haa’í → where: =sh → =INTRG | do’ (nv): also (en): do’ → also]

Reference: Let’s see, I believe it was in the month of October

(REFERENCE 6)
English: I was born on a night when a heavy snow had begun to fall.
1
English: He was a trader.
Navajo: ’Éí naalyéhé ya’ sidáhí jílį́įgo
[’Éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | naalyéhé ya’ sidáhí (nv): trader (en): naalyéhé ya’ sidáhí → trader | jílį́įgo (nv): he was (en): j- → ANIM: í- → TH: lį́į → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB]

2
English: I had a hard time during the flu epidemic.
Navajo: Tah honiigááh diné bitaadaasnii’go ’atah shich’į’ nahwiis’náa ni’
[Tah honiigááh (nv): it becomes feverish (en): tah → among: ho- → AR: n- → TH: ii- → 3.YI.ZERO.IMPF: gááh → become.white.TRNSL.IMPF | diné (nv): people (en): diné → person | bitaadaasnii’go (nv): the epidemic spreads among them (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: taa → among: daa- → DISTR: s- → 3.SI.PERF: nii’ → move.PERF: =go → =SUB | ’atah (nv): among (en): ’a- → NSP.POSS: tah → among | shich’į’ (nv): toward me (en): shi- → 1.SG.POSS: ch’į’ → toward | nahwiis’náa (nv): things have quaked (en): na- → CONT: hw- → AR: ii- → SER: s- → 3.SI.PERF: ’- → D: náa → quake.PERF | ni’ (nv): PST (en): ni’ → PST]

3
English: And since it had snowed the night before they had to go through the snow.
Navajo: Tł’éédą́ą́’ yidzaazgo yas biyi’ ’íldeé jiní
[Tł’éédą́ą́’ (nv): The night before (en): tł’éé’ → night: =dą́ą́’ → =PST | yidzaazgo (nv): it had snowed (en): yi- → 3.YI.PERF: dzaaz → snow.falls.PERF: =go → =SUB | yas (nv): snow (en): yas → snow | biyi’ (nv): in it (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: yi’ → inside.of | ’íldeé (nv): they moved (en): ’- → NSP.SBJ: í- → 3.NI.PERF: l- → L: deé → move.through.air.PL.PERF | jiní (nv): they say (en): ji- → ANIM: ní → say.NEU.IMPF]

4
English: everyone moved.
Navajo: ’Ałtso yiih yíná ’akon
[’Ałtso (nv): all (en): ’ałtso → all | yiih (nv): into it (en): y- → 3.YI.POSS: iih → into | yíná (nv): they moved (en): yí- → 3.NI.PERF: ná → move.PERF | ’akon (nv): so (en): ’akon → so]

5
English: They spent the night beneath a robe.
Navajo: Ch’idí wolyéii t’éiyá hak’i dah siłtsoozgo yiską́ą́ lá jiní
[Ch’idí (nv): robe (en): ch’idí → robe | wolyéii (nv): the one that is called (en): wo- → DIR: l- → L: yé → call.NEU.IMPF: =ii → =PCL.REF | t’éiyá (nv): only (en): t’éiyá → only | hak’i (nv): on it (en): ha- → ANIM.POSS: k’i → on | dah (nv): up (en): dah → up | siłtsoozgo (nv): lying (en): si- → 3.SI.PERF: ł- → Ł: tsooz → lie.FFO.PERF: =go → =SUB | yiską́ą́ (nv): they two spend the night (en): yi- → TH: s- → 3.SI.PERF: ką́ą́ → night.passes.PERF | lá (nv): ! (en): lá → ! | jiní (nv): they say (en): ji- → ANIM: ní → say.NEU.IMPF]

Reference: I was born on a night when a heavy snow had begun to fall.

(REFERENCE 7)
English: My grandfather, a man by the name of White Mexican Clansman, and another man called The Son of Former Sweathouse, were the only men. They and several womenfolk worked hard on me.
1
English: And it turned out that my grandfather, who was called Big Mexican Clansman, was there too.
Navajo: Shichei Naakaii Dine’étsoh wolyéé ńt’éé’ ’éí dó’ sidáá lá
[Shichei (nv): my grandfather (en): shi- → 1.SG.POSS: chei → maternal.grandfather | Naakaii Dine’étsoh (nv): Big Mexican Clansman (en): Naakaii Dine’étsoh → Big.Mexican.Clansman | wolyéé (nv): is called (en): wo- → DIR: l- → L: yéé → call.NEU.IMPF | ńt’éé’ (nv): past (en): ńt’éé’ → PST | ’éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | dó’ (nv): also (en): dó’ → also | sidáá (nv): was sitting (en): si- → 3.SI.PERF: dáá → sit.NEU.PERF | lá (nv): ! (en): lá → !]

2
English: I had an uncle by the name of Forked House. He was a young man, and he adopted me.
Navajo: Shidá’í ńt’éé’ T’iisyaa Bikiní wolyéé ńt’éé’ tsíłkę́ę́h nilį́įgo ’éí ’áyaa nikishidiilt’e’

(REFERENCE 13)

English: When I was perhaps eight years old the people in the whole area hoaxed one another.

[Nááná (nv): again (en): nááná → again | díí (nv): this (en): díí → this | ts’ídá (nv): really (en): ts’ídá → really | daats’í (nv): maybe (en): daats’í → maybe | tseebíí (nv): eight (en): tseebíí → eight | shinááhaigo (nv): I am years old (en): shi- → 1.SG.POSS: ná- → REV: áá- → 3.YI.PERF: hai → winter.passes.PERF: =go → =SUB | ’éiyá (nv): that one (en): ’éiyá → that.one | díí (nv): this (en): díí → this | kéyah (nv): land (en): kéyah → land | nihił (nv): with us (en): nih- → 1.DU/PL.POSS: ił → with | dah (nv): up (en): dah → up | si’ánígíí (nv): it is placed (en): si- → 3.SI.PERF: ’án → move.SRO.PERF: =ígíí → =PRES.REF | bikáa’gi (nv): on top of it (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: káa’ → on.top: =gi → =at.SP | łah (nv): once (en): łah → once | shį́įgo (nv): during the summer (en): shį́į → summer: =go → =SUB | da’ahizhnéét’aa’ (nv): they deceived one another (en): da- → DISTR: ’ahi- → REC: zh- → ANIM: n- → TH: éé- → 3.YI.PERF: t- → D: ’aa’ → deceive.MOM1.PERF | ni’ (nv): PST (en): ni’ → PST]

Navajo: ’Áko ’éí yaa ndilt’éego Bis Dootł’izh Deez’áhí hoolyéegi naalyéhé bá hooghan ’áhoolaa

Reference: At the time I was about eighteen years old the person who started the present trading post at Blue Clay Point moved there - I used to know just when it was.

(REFERENCE 884)

(REFERENCE 1064)

1
English: Even though your children are taught for you in school, we should not lose sight of our teaching.
Navajo: Nihá nanitin dadohníi ndi jó ’akon jó t’áá nihí nihina’nitin ’ałdó’ t’áá ’óóltą’go yá’át’ééh shidine’é ’ałtah ’áásįįłóó
[Nihá (nv): for you (en): nih- → 2.DU/PL.POSS: á → for | nanitin (nv): they are being taught (en): na- → CONT: ni- → TH: tin → move.SSO.CONT.IMP | dadohníi (nv): you say (en): da- → DISTR: d- → TH: oh- → 2.DU/PL.ZERO.IMPF: níi → say.NEU.IMPF | ndi (nv): even (en): ndi → even | jó (nv): well (en): jó → well | ’akon (nv): so (en): ’akon → so | jó (nv): well (en): jó → well | t’áá nihí (nv): our very own (en): t’áá → PCL: nihí → 1.DU/PL | nihina’nitin (nv): our teaching (en): nihi- → 1.DU/PL.POSS: na- → CONT: ’- → NSP.OBJ: ni- → TH: tin → move.SSO.CONT.IMP | ’ałdó’ (nv): also (en): ’ałdó’ → also | t’áá (nv): just (en): t’áá → PCL | ’óóltą’go (nv): they are included with it (en): ’- → NSP.OBJ: óó- → DIR: l- → L: tą’ → hold.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | yá’át’ééh (nv): good (en): yá’át’ééh → good | shidine’é (nv): my people (en): shi- → 1.SG.POSS: dine’é → people | ’ałtah ’áásįįłóó (nv): audience (en): ’ałtah ’áásįįłóó → audience]

2
English: Then I sulked around and didn't eat at all.
Navajo: ’Áko naashchxǫ’go biniinaa t’áadoo ’ííyą́ą’ da
[’Áko (nv): so (en): ’áko → so | naashchxǫ’go (nv): I sulked around (en): naa- → CONT: sh- → 1.SG.ZERO.IMPF: chxǫ’ → mess.up.CONT.IMPF: =go → =SUB | biniinaa (nv): because of it (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: niinaa → reason | t’áadoo (nv): not anymore (en): t’áadoo → not.anymore | ’ííyą́ą’ (nv): I ate (en): ’- → NSP.SBJ: íí- → 1.SG.YI.PERF: yą́ą’ → eat.PERF | da (nv): not (en): da → not]

3
English: Just very recently some hospitals were built, but we do not have enough of them.
Navajo: Jó k’ad ’éiyá t’áá ’ádíyídígo ’azee’ál’į́ ’ádaalyaa ndi t’áá nihi’oh dahonee’ą́ ’akon
[Jó (nv): well (en): jó → well | k’ad (nv): now (en): k’ad → now | ’éiyá (nv): there (en): ’éiyá → that.one | t’áá (nv): very (en): t’áá → PCL | ’ádíyídígo (nv): recently (en): ’ádíyídígo → recently | ’azee’ál’į́ (nv): hospital (en): ’azee’ál’į́ → hospital | ’ádaalyaa (nv): they have been built (en): ’á- → TH: da- → DISTR: aa- → 3.YI.PERF: l- → L: yaa → make.MOM3.PERF.PAS | ndi (nv): but (en): ndi → but | t’áá (nv): just (en): t’áá → PCL | nihi’oh (nv): we’ve got left (en): nihi- → 1.DU/PL.POSS: ’oh → less.than | dahonee’ą́ (nv): they have been placed (en): da- → DISTR: ho- → AR: n- → TH: ee- → 3.NI.PERF: ’ą́ → move.SRO.PERF | ’akon (nv): so (en): ’akon → so]

4
English: We somewhat don’t think for our children.
Navajo: T’áá bíyó ła’ niha’áłchíní doo hózhǫ́ bá ntsídeiikees da ’akon
[T’áá bíyó (nv): somewhat (en): t’áá bíyó → somewhat | ła’ (nv): few (en): ła’ → few | niha’áłchíní (nv): our children (en): niha- → 1.DU/PL.POSS: ’áłchíní → children | doo (nv): not (en): doo → not | hózhǫ́ (nv): nicely (en): hó- → AR.NI.TH: zhǫ́ → beautiful.NEU.IMPF | bá (nv): for them (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: á → for | ntsídeiikees (nv): we think (en): ntsí- → mind: de- → DISTR: ii- → 1.DU/PL.ZERO.IMPF: kees → think.IMPF | da (nv): not (en): da → not | ’akon (nv): so (en): ’akon → so]

5
English: Some of us neglect our children.
Navajo: Jó k’ad dííjį́ ’éiyá t’óó ’ahídayíníilkáá’ nahalingo naha’áłchíní łahgóó doo hózhǫ́ ndaniitin da
[Jó (nv): well (en): jó → well | k’ad (nv): now (en): k’ad → now | dííjį́ (nv): today (en): díí → this: jį́ → day | ’éiyá (nv): then (en): ’éiyá → then | t’óó (nv): just (en): t’óó → just | ’ahídayíníilkáá’ (nv): we are considerate of each other (en): ’ah- → REC.POSS: í → against: da- → DISTR: yí- → TH: níi- → 1.DU/PL.NI.IMPF: l- → L: káá’ → be.on.surface.IMPF | nahalingo (nv): resembling (en): na- → CONT: ha- → AR: lin → resemble.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | naha’áłchíní (nv): our children (en): naha- → 1.DU/PL.POSS: ’áłchíní → children | łahgóó (nv): once (en): łah → once: =góó → =towards | doo (nv): not (en): doo → not | hózhǫ́ (nv): nicely (en): hó- → AR.NI.TH: zhǫ́ → beautiful.NEU.IMPF | ndaniitin (nv): we teach (en): n- → CONT: da- → DISTR: n- → TH: ii- → 1.DU/PL.ZERO.IMPF: tin → move.SSO.CONT.IMP | da (nv): not (en): da → not]

Reference: But today we are lax and do not teach our children much.

(REFERENCE 1065)
English: Some of us neglect our children.
1
English: Many of our womenfolk died.
Navajo: Nihizáanii daashį́į́ néelą́ą́’ ’ádin silį́į́’
[Nihizáanii (nv): our women (en): nihi- → 1.DU/PL.POSS: záanii → women | daashį́į́ néelą́ą́’ (nv): I wonder how many (en): daashį́į́ néelą́ą́’ → I.wonder.how.many | ’ádin (nv): there is not (en): ’á- → TH: din → vanish.NEU.IMPF | silį́į́’ (nv): they have become (en): si- → 3.SI.PERF: lį́į́’ → be.PERF]

2
English: God punished us.
Navajo: God wolyéii ’atínihiilaa
[God (nv): God (en): God → God | wolyéii (nv): the one that is called (en): wo- → DIR: l- → L: yé → call.NEU.IMPF: =ii → =PCL.REF | ’atínihiilaa (nv): he punished us (en): ’atí- → suffering: nih- → 1.DU/PL: ii- → 3.YI.ZERO.PERF: laa → make.MOM3.PERF.PAS]

3
English: Some of your mothers are not living.
Navajo: Ła’ ’éí nihimá ’ádaadin
[Ła’ (nv): few (en): ła’ → few | ’éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | nihimá (nv): your mother (en): nihi- → 2.DU/PL.POSS: má → mother | ’ádaadin (nv): they are not (en): ’á- → TH: daa- → DISTR: din → vanish.NEU.IMPF]

4
English: But today we are lax and do not teach our children much.
Navajo: Yéego na’anitin yee ’ałch’į’ yádaalti’go kót’éego ła’ tsíłkéí yá’át’éhgo biyaa dahoo’a’
[Yéego (nv): diligently (en): yéego → diligently | na’anitin (nv): teaching (en): na- → CONT: ’a- → NSP.OBJ: ni- → TH: tin → move.SSO.CONT.IMP | yee (nv): about it (en): y- → 3.YI.POSS: ee → about | ’ałch’į’ (nv): toward one another (en): ’ał- → REC.POSS: ch’į’ → toward | yádaalti’go (nv): when they speak to one another (en): yá- → TH: daa- → DISTR: l- → L: ti’ → speak.DUR.IMPF: =go → =SUB | kót’éego (nv): it is thus (en): kót’ée → it.is.thus: =go → =SUB | ła’ (nv): few (en): ła’ → few | tsíłkéí (nv): young men (en): tsíłkéí → young.men | yá’át’éhgo (nv): well (en): yá’át’éh → good: =go → =SUB | biyaa dahoo’a’ (nv): they grow up (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: yaa → under: da- → DISTR: h- → AR: oo- → 3.YI.ZERO.PERF: ’a’ → move.SRO.TRNSL.PERF]

5
English: Eight of us died.
Navajo: Tseebíí ’éí ’ádaadin daazlį́į́’
[Tseebíí (nv): eight (en): tseebíí → eight | ’éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | ’ádaadin (nv): they vanish (en): ’á- → TH: daa- → DISTR: din → vanish.NEU.IMPF | daazlį́į́’ (nv): they were (en): daa- → DISTR: z- → 3.SI.PERF: lį́į́’ → be.PERF]

Reference: Some of us neglect our children.

(REFERENCE 1067)
English: It’s well doable for us to teach our children those things
1
English: So then it’s good that one recalls those stories, those things that happened. That’s how you have memories.
Navajo: ’Áko yá’át’ééh ’ákót’éego ńléídę́ę́’ dahane’ danilíinii yę́ę ’índa łahgóó ’ádahóót’įįdii nát’ą́ą́’ hoł níjólta’go jó ’áko ’éí bee háni’ hooleeł łeh ’akon
[’Áko (nv): so (en): ’áko → so | yá’át’ééh (nv): good (en): yá’át’ééh → good | ’ákót’éego (nv): while it being so (en): ’ákó- → so: t’ée → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | ńléídę́ę́’ (nv): from over there (en): ńléí → over.there: =dę́ę́’ → =from | dahane’ (nv): stories (en): da- → DISTR: hane’ → story | danilíinii (nv): the ones that are (en): da- → DISTR: ni- → TH: líin → be.NEU.IMPF: =ii → =PCL.REF | yę́ę (nv): PAST (en): =yę́ę → =PST.REF | ’índa (nv): then (en): ’índa → then | łahgóó (nv): once (en): łah → once: =góó → =towards | ’ádahóót’įįdii (nv): the things that happened (en): ’á- → TH: da- → DISTR: h- → AR: óó- → 3.YI.PERF: t- → D: ’įįd → do.PERF: =ii → =PCL.REF | nát’ą́ą́’ (nv): returning back (en): nát’ą́ą́’ → back | hoł (nv): with one (en): ha- → ANIM.POSS: ł → with | níjólta’go (nv): one keeps recalling (en): ní- → REP: j- → ANIM: ó- → DIR: l- → L: ta’ → count.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | jó (nv): well (en): jó → well | ’áko (nv): so (en): ’áko → so | ’éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | bee (nv): by means of (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: ee → with | háni’ (nv): one’s mind (en): h- → ANIM.POSS: áni’ → mind | hooleeł (nv): it comes into being (en): h- → AR: oo- → 3.PROG: leeł → become.PROG | łeh (nv): usually (en): łeh → usually | ’akon (nv): so (en): ’akon → so]

2
English: That's the way children grow up.
Navajo: ’Éí ’áájí ’ákót’éego jiinaah
[’Éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | ’áájí (nv): then (en): ’áá → then: =jí → =away | ’ákót’éego (nv): while it being so (en): ’ákó- → so: t’ée → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | jiinaah (nv): they grow up (en): j- → ANIM: ii- → TH: naah → live.IMPF]

3
English: I am now telling these stories of mine to your children.
Navajo: Díidíígíí shahane’ígíí k’ad nizhónígo ’áłchíníkǫ́ǫ́ bee bił ndahashne’
[Díidíígíí (nv): this one (en): díidí → this.one: =ígíí → =PRES.REF | shahane’ígíí (nv): my story (en): sha- → 1.SG.POSS: hane’ → story: =ígíí → =PRES.REF | k’ad (nv): now (en): k’ad → now | nizhónígo (nv): nicely (en): ni- → TH: zhóní → beautiful.ABS.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | ’áłchíníkǫ́ǫ́ (nv): the children here (en): ’áłchíní → children: kǫ́ǫ́ → through.here | bee (nv): about it (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: ee → about | bił (nv): to them (en): bi- → 3.BI.POSS: ił → with | ndahashne’ (nv): I am telling stories (en): n- → CONT: da- → DISTR: ha- → AR: sh- → 1.SG.ZERO.IMPF: ne’ → tell.DUR.IMPF]

4

English: That is how the dog protected me.
Navajo: ’Ákót’éego łééchąą’í shich’ą́ą́h naayáa ni’
[’Ákót’éego (nv): while it being so (en): ’ákó- → so: t’ée → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | łééchąą’í (nv): dog (en): łééchąą’í → dog | shich’ą́ą́h (nv): in front of me (en): shi- → 1.SG.POSS: ch’ą́ą́h → in.front.of | naayáa (nv): he went about (en): naa- → CONT: s- → 3.SI.PERF: yáa → go.SG.PERF | ni’ (nv): PST (en): ni’ → PST]

2
English: This is how my people suffered.
Navajo: Jó ’akon ’ákót’éego shidine’é ’atíbi’diilyaa
[Jó (nv): well (en): jó → well | ’akon (nv): so (en): ’akon → so | ’ákót’éego (nv): while it being so (en): ’ákó- → so: t’ée → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | shidine’é (nv): my people (en): shi- → 1.SG.POSS: dine’é → people | ’atíbi’diilyaa (nv): they were injured (en): ’atí- → suffering: bi- → 3.BI: ’d- → AGT.PAS: ii- → 3.YI.ZERO.PERF: l- → L: yaa → make.MOM3.PERF.PAS]

3

4

[’Éí (nv): that (en): ’éí → that | ’ałdó’ (nv): also (en): ’ałdó’ → also | t’áá (nv): just (en): t’áá → PCL | ńléí (nv): over there (en): ńléí → over.there | halgaidi (nv): in the plain (en): halgai → plain: =di → =at.GEN]

2
English: Then things really happened.
Navajo: ’Aadóó hááhgóó shį́į́ ’áhóót’įįd
[’Aadóó (nv): and then (en): ’aadóó → and.then | hááhgóó shį́į́ (nv): hard (en): hááhgóóshį́į́ → really | ’áhóót’įįd (nv): it happened (en): ’á- → TH: h- → AR: óó- → 3.YI.PERF: t- → D: ’įįd → do.PERF]

3
English: It happened that way.
Navajo: Kódzaa jó ’akon
[Kódzaa (nv): thus it happened (en): kó- → so: dzaa → do.PERF | jó (nv): well (en): jó → well | ’akon (nv): so (en): ’akon → so]

4
English: That happened to two of us.
Navajo: Ndiilt’éego ’ákwíidzaa lá
[Ndiilt’éego (nv): two of us (en): n- → two: d- → TH: ii- → 1.DU/PL.ZERO.IMPF: l- → L: t’ée → be.NEU.IMPF: =go → =SUB | ’ákwíidzaa (nv): it happened (en): ’ákwíi- → so: dzaa → do.PERF | lá (nv): ! (en): lá → !]

5
English: It happened thus.
Navajo: Kót’é
[Kót’é (nv): so (en): kó- → so: t’é → be.NEU.IMPF]

Reference: It happened eighteen years ago too.

(REFERENCE 1429)
English: That's the way it is.
1

3
English: "It's a fact.

[T’áá lá ’aaníí (nv): It is the truth (en): t’áá lá ’aaníí → it.is.the.truth | ni (nv): you (en): ni → surely]

4
English: That's the Navajo way.

5
English: That's the way children grow up.
Navajo: ’Éí ’áájí ’ákót’éego jiinaah
